status: wired (2026-07-18): manifest ops + SDK tools live in the shared core public/js/sdk-core.js (this CLI re-exports them); SDK mode (pipeline runSdkBuild) and the /mcp sdk_* tools consume this manifest from the deployed snapshot; generated builds publish at /app/<slug>/

classes.C: client-pure — no server in the data path; must work on a static host
classes.S: server-backed — lives in the one server component
classes.B: bridged — server capability lent to the client tier via metered grant tokens
classes.X: shared substrate — one pure core under the client tree, server façade re-export
classes.D: development system — loops/evals/workflows that keep the pair maintainable

modules.name: The agent-pair architecture and its contracts
modules.provides: The pair abstraction (client tier + server tier, zero-or-one server), the capability classes C/S/B/X/D, contracts PA-1..PA-10, the naming/wordplay convention, module-graph constraints
modules.acceptance: Every later module cites the PA contracts it touches; class-C module graphs contain no class-S imports (pinned by tests)

modules.name: The one server component (edge worker skeleton)
modules.provides: Entrypoint routing, static-asset serving + public allowlist, security headers/CSP, canonical-origin redirect, optional-D1 + lazy schema, site config, structured logging, http helpers, node:test harness, zero-build typecheck
modules.acceptance: npm test green on the skeleton; serves the client shell; headers verified with a live probe; works with NO D1 binding

modules.name: The browser chat client shell
modules.provides: No-build ES-module client: glass chrome, composer, conversation turns, sanitized markdown, SSE line parser, history sidebar, settings client, attachments, the pure-core (Node-testable) module convention
modules.acceptance: Pure cores import-safe in Node with tests; page renders and round-trips a mocked chat

modules.name: LLM provider registry and stream consumption
modules.provides: Namespace-dispatched multi-provider catalog (server: providers.js pattern; client: CORS-capable + local registry), wire adapters (adapt-at-the-wire SSE vs native), key gating, pricing metadata, the hardened stream loop (idle/total guards, finish_reason, retries)
modules.acceptance: Unit + mock-HTTP smoke per provider; live probe per key-gated provider; catalog degrades when a key is absent

modules.name: The deterministic deep-research pipeline
modules.provides: Triage → search → gap → synthesis → validation with NO function calling; fail-soft phases; split model routing; time-budget/EWMA planner; report tiers; JSON hardening (schema validator + normalizers); per-model profiles; the client-side port of the same flow
modules.acceptance: Flow unit-tested end-to-end against a mock provider on BOTH tiers; PA-1/2/3 asserted by tests

modules.name: Web search plane (default + pluggable backends)
modules.provides: Default search provider client (Exa pattern), the pluggable-backend shared core (SearXNG/self-hosted, browser-direct on the client tier), edge result cache, the numbered source registry with per-origin diversity caps, auxiliary search-source registry (HF Hub pattern)
modules.acceptance: Registry/diversity unit tests; mocked backend dispatch tests; one live search probe; fallback-to-default verified

modules.name: Opt-in context enrichment registry
modules.provides: The enrichment contract (deterministic target extraction → bounded privacy-minimal lookup → capped summary → ONE labeled context block → fail soft → counted), the registry seam, worked integrations (geocode, Shodan, Maps incl. vision describe), EN+SV intent-gate parity
modules.acceptance: Pure gates/builders unit-tested with language parity; live probe per integration; chat never errors when an enrichment fails

modules.name: The client tier (Se/cure archetype)
modules.provides: The wholly-in-browser assistant: chat-first UX, browser-direct providers incl. keyless local server, sealed project state in browser storage, the twin look-and-feel with dimmed server-only affordances, deep links, encrypted backup files, the minimal-server-by-design page
modules.acceptance: Works served from a static host; no authenticated endpoint in the module graph (test-pinned); state round-trips sealed

modules.name: Identity, accounts and the admin gate
modules.provides: OIDC sign-in (Google pattern), session cookies (sliding, HMAC), terms + approval gates, auto-provisioned accounts, break-glass Basic Auth (fail closed), the admin surface gate, PWA-longevity decisions
modules.acceptance: Cookie/HMAC security properties unit-tested (no-secret fails closed); live sign-in round-trip; grant tokens can never pass the identity gate

modules.name: Quotas, usage accounting and alerts
modules.provides: Multi-window quota model, split-billing cost math per provider catalog rate, usage recording, per-user in-flight concurrency caps, 429 payload conventions, operational alerts (typed, upserted), the per-user message center (metadata-only)
modules.acceptance: Window/boundary math unit-tested; breach + concurrency paths tested; alert classification tested

modules.name: Chat transport: SSE contract + answer recovery
modules.provides: The /api/chat SSE event vocabulary + forward-compatibility rule, heartbeats, the TTL'd answer-recovery cache, the client poller, the resume-across-relaunch metadata-only marker, stall watchdogs, stop-mid-stream
modules.acceptance: SSE parser + recovery projections unit-tested; disconnect/recover verified live (only reproduces in production)

modules.name: Sealed client-side crypto core
modules.provides: User-held secrets (copy-safe Crockford base32, forgiving normalization), HKDF-independent derivations (ids vs keys), AES-256-GCM archives with tamper detection, the master-secret profile (keys INSIDE the sealed state), password-manager-compatible secret forms
modules.acceptance: Determinism/independence/tamper suites green in Node; no plaintext key material at rest anywhere in the module

modules.name: Cloud ciphertext storage and the vault tier
modules.provides: Knob-gated R2 storage of encrypted conversations/projects/files, the encryption asymmetry rule (RAG-indexed plaintext exception, declared), server-derived in-memory history keys, the blind-blob vault (server never sees the secret), full drain-wipe semantics
modules.acceptance: Vault round-trip vs mocked R2; works-with-knob-OFF pinned; vault excluded from drain-wipe; ciphertext-only at rest verified

modules.name: Client-side RAG over documents, projects and chats
modules.provides: Chunking/cosine/top-k pure core, embedding via server proxy (server tier) or browser-direct (client tier), incremental chat indexing, sibling-scope retrieval, recall blocks as context-not-instructions, vectors sealed at rest on the client tier, cap/eviction policies
modules.acceptance: Chunker/codec/scope suites green; embedder-change wipe tested; recall block threaded into pipeline phases (never harvest) tested

modules.name: The grant-token bridge between tiers
modules.provides: Token families under one root secret with structural namespace separation; the consolidated JWT server token (perms set, per-permission meter rows, THE GUARANTEE: upstream APIs only, never server data, never a login); atomic reserve/refund meters; global budget ceilings; ghost-crossover + shareable-link minting; admin governance panels; quota adjust/pause/revoke
modules.acceptance: The full quota-grant invariant checklist green (cross-family forgery matrix, concurrency overrun, refund floors, expiry, budget, account binding, module-graph pin) over a combined D1 fake

modules.name: Offline workspace links (fragment crypto)
modules.provides: A fully configured client-tier session sealed into one link (fragment never reaches a server), the iterative-KDF + dual-key split wire format, embedded grant tokens that stay minter-administered, reserved slugs, the share/mint UX on both tiers
modules.acceptance: Seal→open round-trip + tamper/wrong-password fail-soft green; end-to-end mint→seal→open→hydrate→spend→pause→revoke flow tested

modules.name: Observability, interaction logs and live verification
modules.provides: Structured JSON logging, request-id correlation end to end, the full-visibility interaction log with per-conversation incognito opt-out (a declared PA-4 exception), client error/telemetry beacons, admin read APIs + CLI, the live-verify and on-device-trace disciplines
modules.acceptance: Log row shape/truncation/opt-out unit-tested; a live request traced by id through worker logs; no secrets in any log (scanned)

modules.name: Admin decision boards feeding agent loops
modules.provides: The shared board core (votes/score/note/priority state, priority-vs-rank orderings, review upserts), catalog/façade/mirror disciplines, priority boards + the votes-only attention-board variant, ?format=text loop input + per-board CLIs, the boards discovery index
modules.acceptance: Core + each consumer's suites green; catalog mirrors its register file in the same commit (test-named drift); a loop can consume ?format=text

modules.name: User feedback and testing loops
modules.provides: Per-reply feedback threads with screenshots, the testable-interaction-points queue (try-it deep links, action grammar, three-verdict dialogue), the git-transported test-request channel, standing test batteries, the human-in-the-loop discipline
modules.acceptance: Pure validation/projection suites green; a point mints → deep-links → records a verdict end to end

modules.name: Model evaluation and scored benchmarks
modules.provides: The model-matrix trace harness, the LLM-judged rubric bench, the external gold-answer bench (contamination-aware), append-only findings ledgers, the evidence-before-override rule for model profiles, don't-deploy-mid-battery
modules.acceptance: A baseline battery runs and lands in a ledger before any pipeline-quality change merges

modules.name: The agent development system
modules.provides: Slim-memory + on-demand skills structure, sync-main/merge-barrier/branch-ledger git discipline, PR watching + regression routing to author-workers, maintenance ownership registry, secret scanning hooks, docs-mirror drift tests, persist-solved-tasks-as-skills
modules.acceptance: Hooks fire on session start/commit/push; a regression routes to its owning PR; skills list matches the skills directory

modules.name: In-browser Linux execution sandbox + bash agent
modules.provides: WASM x86 Linux in the browser (cross-origin-isolated), the client-orchestrated fenced-block shell loop (no function calling), the shared bash core + server step endpoint, exec bridge envelope codec, file mounts + outbox deliverables, boot observability
modules.acceptance: Pure core suites green incl. EN+SV parity; live browser boot + one command round-trip verified on a real device

modules.name: Self-introspection and interactive help
modules.provides: Committed source snapshot + dense int8 RAG index (delta builds, freshness test-enforced), the shared intent/retrieval core, docs corpus as the help layer (verbatim quoting, images, symbol refs), the scoped dev-mode tool-loop exception to PA-1, the skills catalog surfaced to answer models
modules.acceptance: Freshness tests fail npm test on stale artifacts; both tiers answer a how-are-you-built question from real source

modules.name: The pair as an MCP server
modules.provides: Hand-rolled JSON-RPC 2.0 / Streamable HTTP endpoint exposing the pipeline as one tool; pure-protocol-static / pipeline-dynamic import layout; inherits identity gate, quota and billing; the outbound-edge-only MCP stance
modules.acceptance: Protocol suite green without importing the pipeline; live JSON-RPC probe completes a tool call with usage recorded

modules.name: Published research replays
modules.provides: Frozen research sessions as public read-only pages under the client tier's path (slug completes the wordplay), admin-only publish, the continue-on-own-keys handoff seeding a client-tier conversation in place
modules.acceptance: Slug rules + round-trip suites green vs mocked R2; a replay opens and continues on visitor keys live

modules.name: Symbol language, wordmark and UX conventions
modules.provides: Per-tier symbol characters and palettes, first-visit intros + waiting-symbol grammar (offline vs online channels, disclosure notices), the wordmark slash-spacing measurement discipline, the numbered UX-conventions registry, low-animation directives
modules.acceptance: Pure timeline/geometry cores tested; slash gaps measured not eyeballed; every online phase carries its disclosure

modules.name: The games registry seam
modules.provides: The declarative game registry + dispatch (a new game touches no shelf code), the pure-core/API/client split, server-authoritative rules with anti-cheat view projections, the no-invented-game-logic rule
modules.acceptance: Registry dispatch suites green; a second registered game appears on the shelf with zero client changes

modules.name: The in-browser execution engine, built from source
modules.provides: Engine CheerpX (decided; proprietary, i386, not source-built — accepted). Building OUR OWN small Linux image from scratch (reproducible Alpine-i386 recipe, tools pinned + added as we go), self-hosted same-origin, SMALL + FAST + full-prefetch so it loads quickly and commands never stall fetching hundreds of MB. Plus the thin engine-agnostic ExecEngine seam + the c2w/v86/qemu fallback ladder as optional future-proofing, and the in-VM agent egress design (host-shim keeps keys/egress out of the VM; metered relay via the grant bridge only for genuine internet needs)
modules.acceptance: Small Alpine-i386 image builds reproducibly (all binaries Intel 80386), served same-origin with Range, boots on Chrome/Firefox/real iOS Safari; loads fast and after prefetch a second boot + commands issue ZERO network block reads (no stalls); adding a tool = edit recipe + rebuild + new id; bash-core loop still passes against a mock engine

modules.name: Fast-track workspace filesystem (host file ops, VM-routed shell)
modules.provides: The answer model does read/write/edit/grep/glob/list through MCP tools hitting a host-side authoritative store DIRECTLY (no VM round-trip); only arbitrary shell routes through the CheerpX VM, kept coherent by a sync-in→exec→harvest protocol at each run_bash boundary (DataDevice.writeFile in, IDBDevice.readFileAsBlob out, hash-gated so only changed files cross); the store (Durable Object / R2) with a generation counter + exec-serialized writes. Server-tier first; Se/cure deferred/unsuitable (no MCP-client model, no server host)
modules.acceptance: File tools work with NO VM boot; a host-side edit is exactly what run_bash sees (sync-in) and a bash write is what the next host read returns (harvest); only changed files cross; host writes serialize against an in-flight exec; VM-down degrades to host-only file ops; no store backing → unavailable not unmetered; coherence cycle live-verified on a real device

modules.name: Deploy the workspace and try it live
modules.provides: A deploy tool + build hook turning a workspace into a running thing the user can open: a live same-origin preview URL for a client-tier (static) build, or a push to the USER'S OWN edge account for a server-tier build (the pair never hosts generated server code on its own origin); the deploy credential ridden through the grant discipline (minimal, user-held, unlogged, revocable); content-addressed build ids with rollback; the live-URL return. Server-tier
modules.acceptance: A client-tier build deploys to a live same-origin URL openable in-session; a server-tier build publishes to the user's own account and the pair's origin serves no generated server code; the deploy token is never logged and is revocable; failed build/deploy is fail-soft; re-deploy rolls forward with the old id still openable; no target → unavailable; the live URL is verified by opening it

modules.name: The SDK inside the prepackaged Linux VM
modules.provides: Self-hosted small Linux image built from our own recipe (Alpine-i386 default — small + fast; CheerpX engine i386; archlinux32 selectable but heavy), the R2 Range streamer + config registry + per-image caches, the full-prefetch loads-in-its-entirety mode so commands never stall, the SDK mounted in-VM at /src/sdk with nodejs for pair-cli, the in-app skills catalog namespaced as sdk/<name>, desktop parity
modules.acceptance: Image plumbing inert without a selection; arch32 image boots verified on real devices incl. iOS Safari; node /src/sdk/pair-cli.mjs validate passes in-VM; sdk/<name> entries in the in-app catalog (test-pinned); prefetch makes second boots network-free

modules.name: The in-app pair builder (prompt → VM design → same-UI try-out)
modules.provides: Studio conversation mode driving SDK-guided generation in the sandbox VM; platform types (client-tier builds instantly preview-deployable in an in-app pane via a reserved-scope service worker; server-tier builds export as deployable bundles with mocked-API client preview); save as runnable test application (download / project / sealed link); the never-host-generated-server-code rule
modules.acceptance: Prompt → generated client-tier app previews in-pane in the same session on both tiers; preview survives COEP on real devices; exported bundle runs from a plain static host; S-class modules refused in client-tier selections; no code path hosts generated server code

modules.name: Generating a pair from a feature selection
modules.provides: The selection → dependency closure → layer-ordered, module-at-a-time generation workflow; acceptance gating between modules; the adoption path for wiring an existing product to SDK modules; contract enforcement (no class violations)
modules.acceptance: A minimal selection generates to a working product with all landed modules' acceptance checklists satisfied
